REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS HUONG (AMY) THAI-SILVA, VINH THAI, MUOI QUACH'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, NUMBER 9.2 AND 15.1; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND HIS ATTORNEY, TANNER BRINK, IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,067. THIS MATTER IS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED RE (1) FORM INTERROGATORY 9.2 FOR DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE A SUPPLEMENTAL VERIFICATION STATING THAT ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN PRODUCED AND IDENTIFY THE DOCUMENTS BY BATES NUMBER; (2) FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1 FOR DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTAL VERIFIED RESPONSES REASONABLY DIRECTED TO EACH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES AND EACH DENIAL OF MATERIAL ALLEGATIONS. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES HAINES; CLERK: J. BERTELSEN; NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Nov-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Courtney M. Brown, esq. (415-371-9114) for Plaintiffs/Moving party HUONG (AMY) THAI-SILVA, VINH THAI, MUOI QUACH.
Tanner D. Brink, esq. (925-9449700 x404) of Buchman Provine Brothers Smith LLP for Defendant/opposing party Minh Thai via Court Call.

Ms. Brown ARGUED re: substantial justification to file motion, good faith belief defendant did not search all documents, made meet and confer efforts. Mr. Tanner ARGUED re: not relevant/ substantial justification for filing motion, his client incurred over $3,000.00 in costs due to this lawsuit and should be compensated. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs HUONG (AMY) THAI-SILVA, VINH THAI, MUOI QUACH's Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories, Set One, Number 6; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And Their Attorney, Tannner, Brink, In The Amount Of $942 is DENIED. Defendant served a timely code-complaint response to Special Interrogatory 6. Defendant's request for sanctions granted in the amount of $1,000 to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of order. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Aug-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS HUONG (AMY) THAI-SILVA, VINH THAI, MUOI QUACH'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, NUMBER 9.2 AND 15.1; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND HIS ATTORNEY, TANNER BRINK, IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,067.

Appearances:
Mary Catherine Wiederhold (415-533-0735) for Plaintiff and moving party.
Tanner D. Brink of Buchman Provine Brothers Smith LLP (925-944-9700) for Defendant and responding party.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:
GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted Re (1) Form Interrogatory 9.2 for Defendant to provide a supplemental verification stating that all responsive documents have been produced and identify the documents by Bates number; (2) Form Interrogatory 15.1 for Defendant to provide supplemental verified responses reasonably directed to each affirmative defenses and each denial of material allegations. Request for sanctions is denied.
Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles Haines; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Not reported. (501/CFH)
